_note: EXAMPLE ONLY — not a deployment record. Shows what a consuming app should derive and assert from deployments/sepolia/stablecoin-vault-simulator.json before surfacing any simulator feature. Values are taken from the live Sepolia record at the time this example was written; always read the canonical source file, not this example.

appGates._description: Boolean assertions the app MUST evaluate before enabling any write interaction with the simulator. All must be true.

disclosureToDisplay: TESTNET ONLY — Ethereum Sepolia. No mainnet deployment exists or is planned.
simulatorLabel: Stablecoin Vault Simulator (testnet research prototype, not audited)
consumptionRules: Read deployments/sepolia/stablecoin-vault-simulator.json as static metadata from the canonical walletwall-vault repository.
Assert tokenMode === 'mock' before any write interaction. Reject anything else.
Assert chainId === 11155111 (Sepolia) or the correct testnet chain ID before any write.
Assert stablecoinVaultSimulatorAddress is non-null before any write interaction.
Show at least one string from the warnings[] array to the user before any write interaction.
Perform on-chain checks (e.g. contract.paused(), contract.token()) before any write — metadata alone does not confirm reachability.
Never label the token as 'USDC' — always 'mUSDC' or 'mock USDC (no monetary value)'.
Never imply yield, interest, APY, APR, returns, rewards, payout, or profit of any kind.
Never imply custody, production-grade security, audited status, or mainnet availability.
Never imply on-chain ML-DSA or quantum-proof verification — the PQ gate is a mock.
Never store or re-publish contract addresses without linking to walletwall-vault as the canonical source.
Metadata presence does NOT mean the simulator is reachable, funded, or ready for transactions.
